text_blocks.snippet: Reunification counselling services in Vancouver, British Columbia, focus on repairing, rebuilding, and strengthening relationships between parents/caregivers and children following separation, high-conflict divorce, or child protection involvement.
Here are key providers and services available in Vancouver and the Lower Mainland:

list.snippet: Reconnect Families (Vancouver/Surrey/Fraser Valley): A team of experienced Marriage and Family Therapists and Counsellors providing specialized services for families with child resistance to visitation, estrangement, or alienation.
Focus: Intensive weekends, in-home outreach, and child-centered therapy.
Contact: Bob Finlay, MA, RCC (Case Manager) - 604-780-7945.

REACH Reunification Program (Dr. Susan Gamache): Focuses on high-conflict separation/divorce and parent-child relationship disruption (alienation/estrangement). They offer team-based therapeutic support.
Bright Star Counselling (Port Moody): Offers a reunification program for families dealing with distance after separation, with a focus on play-based sessions and individualized parent coaching.

Leanne Toews - Family Reunification & Parent-Child Contact: Provides a structured, phased approach (individual sessions, facilitated virtual contact, and in-person sessions) to re-establish connections.
Alyson Jones & Associates - Family Forward: Offers reunification therapy and counseling to assist with blending families and rebuilding bonds. Bright Star Counselling +5

text_blocks.snippet: These programs are typically funded by the Ministry of Children and Family Development (MCFD) for families with active MCFD files due to child protection concerns, or where children are in care.

list.snippet: Vancouver Family Preservation & Reunification Services (Westcoast Family Centres): Offers intensive in-home support, therapeutic supervised access, and counseling to help children return to their families or prevent out-of-home placement.
Touchstone Family Association: Provides in-home/office counselling, parent-teen mediation, and crisis intervention.
Family Preservation and Reunification Counselling Services (North Shore): Offers counseling to families with children experiencing high conflict or challenges. 211 British Columbia +4

Family Justice Centres (BC Government): Provide assistance with family law matters, including mediation and needs assessments. These services are available to help manage disputes that impact child access.
Andrea Barclay - Reunification Therapy: Offers structured, court-involved reunification therapy. www.gov.bc.ca +1
Referral Basis: Many of the community-based services, such as those provided through Westcoast Family Centres, require a direct referral from an MCFD social worker.
Waitlists: Some specialized private services may have waitlists or require intensive intake processes.

Focus: Successful reunification therapy often involves focusing on the parents' behavior and ability to manage conflict rather than focusing solely on the child. Family Services of Greater Vancouver +2
